name: Musings of a Shameless Noble
author: Lady Citta Hothemer of Waterdeep
description:
Musings of a Shameless Noble — A text that names names — or appears to — and has outlived more than one owner. Lady Citta Hothemer of Waterdeep is credited in library catalogues; the work is dated to 1357 DR.

Several entries end mid-sentence, as if the author learned something and chose discretion over royalties.

name: My Dinner With Goblins
author: Angus Hammerfall
description:
My Dinner With Goblins — A popular tale in print — performed in taverns before it was ever bound between covers. Angus Hammerfall is credited in library catalogues; the work is dated to before 1372 DR.

Critics call it low art; innkeepers call it excellent for business on rainy nights.

name: Maritime Tales of Faerûn
author: Unknown
description:
Maritime Tales of Faerûn — An in-universe work listed in Candlekeep indices and frontier libraries throughout the Heartlands. Library catalogues list no certain author; the work is dated to before 1492 DR.

Copies vary by scriptorium; Candlekeep treats the title as known though completeness is never guaranteed.

name: Missives of Candlekeep
author: Various
description:
Missives of Candlekeep — A chronicle preserved in northern and coastal scriptoria — dates, names, and the sort of detail merchants pretend not to need. Various is credited in library catalogues; the work is dated to before 1492 DR.

Later hands added marginal dates and crossed out names; historians still argue which revisions were propaganda.

name: Manual of puissant skill at arms
author: Various
description:
Manual of puissant skill at arms — Military history and counsel from campaigns fought along the Frontier and the Sword Coast. Various is credited in library catalogues; the work is dated to various.

Veterans annotate the margins with corrections; green officers treat those notes as insults until the first ambush.

name: Manual of stealthy pilfering
author: Various
description:
Manual of stealthy pilfering — A text that names names — or appears to — and has outlived more than one owner. Various is credited in library catalogues; the work is dated to various.

Several entries end mid-sentence, as if the author learned something and chose discretion over royalties.

name: My Adventures in the Realms
author: Dathlyr "The Hammer" Greybold
description:
My Adventures in the Realms — A popular tale in print — performed in taverns before it was ever bound between covers. Dathlyr "The Hammer" Greybold is credited in library catalogues; the work is dated to unknown.

Critics call it low art; innkeepers call it excellent for business on rainy nights.

name: Meanderings
author: Bowgentle
description:
Meanderings — Practical notes for wayfarers — tolls, seasons, inns, and the unwritten rules of safe passage. Bowgentle is credited in library catalogues; the work is dated to 1231 DR.

Experienced caravans treat the counsel as suggestion only — but rookies who ignore it seldom return to disagree.

name: My Journeys Around the Sea of Fallen Stars
author: Nelve Harssad of Tsurlagol
description:
My Journeys Around the Sea of Fallen Stars — Practical notes for wayfarers — tolls, seasons, inns, and the unwritten rules of safe passage. Nelve Harssad of Tsurlagol is credited in library catalogues; the work is dated to 1298 DR.

Experienced caravans treat the counsel as suggestion only — but rookies who ignore it seldom return to disagree.
